meta_title: Roofing in Davie, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Expert roofing in Davie, FL. Hurricane-rated CertainTeed shingles & West Lake tile. Licensed, insured, A+ BBB. Free estimates. Financing from $87/mo.
h1: Davie's Trusted Roofing Experts: Hurricane-Rated Systems Built to Last
intro:
Davie, Florida is one of Broward County's most desirable communities — a place where tree-lined streets wind through neighborhoods like Rolling Hills, Long Lake Ranches, and Arrowhead, and where homes regularly command values around $460,000 or more. But beneath that quiet, suburban charm lies a serious responsibility: keeping your roof storm-ready in one of the most hurricane-prone regions in the entire country. At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we understand what Davie homeowners are up against, and we've built our roofing services around exactly what this town needs.

Davie sits approximately eight miles from the Atlantic coastline, which means it escapes the most intense coastal surge effects — but it does not escape the wind. The Town of Davie falls within a 170 mph design wind speed zone under the Florida Building Code, making proper roofing materials and installation methods not just a smart investment, but a legal requirement. Neighborhoods like Shenandoah and Ivanhoe contain a significant share of homes built before 1994 — before modern hurricane codes were adopted following Hurricane Andrew — meaning roughly 40% of Davie's housing stock was constructed under older, less stringent standards. If your home falls into that category, a full roof replacement isn't just overdue; it's essential.

From the equestrian estates near the Bergeron Rodeo Grounds to the family homes surrounding Nova Southeastern University and the lush green corridors near Tree Tops Park, Davie's architecture is as diverse as its community. That diversity demands roofing expertise that can handle everything from classic architectural shingles to premium concrete and clay tile systems. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing brings that expertise directly to your door — with in-house crews, proven products, and a track record of over 500 completed homes across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties. We aren't just another subcontractor-heavy operation. We are the Kings of Service, and Davie homeowners deserve nothing less.

section_why.h2: Why Davie Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Every Roofing Project
section_why.body:
Choosing a roofing contractor in South Florida isn't something you should do lightly. The stakes are high — your home, your family's safety, and an investment worth nearly half a million dollars on average. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has earned the trust of Davie residents by doing something that's increasingly rare in this industry: we handle 100% of our roofing installations in-house. No subcontractors, no crews you've never met, no accountability gaps. Every nail, every membrane, every ridge cap is installed by our own trained professionals.

With over 60 years of combined experience across our team, we've seen what South Florida weather does to roofs — and we've spent decades perfecting the systems that stop it. Our project managers are intimately familiar with Broward County's permit requirements, the Town of Davie Building Division's inspection process, and the specific wind load calculations that apply to homes throughout ZIP codes like 33326, 33330, and 33331. That local knowledge translates directly into fewer delays, cleaner inspections, and installations that hold up when the next major storm makes landfall.

We've earned an A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau, and we maintain our licensing and insurance in full compliance with Florida state law — so you're protected from the first consultation to the final inspection. We also offer multiple financing options starting as low as $87 per month with $0 down, making it possible for Davie homeowners to invest in a quality roof replacement without draining savings or waiting until damage gets worse.

Perhaps most importantly, we treat every job — whether it's a modest shingle repair or a full tile roof replacement on a Long Lake Ranches estate — with the same level of professionalism and attention to detail. Our reputation was built one Davie roof at a time, and we intend to keep it that way.

section_history.h2: Davie's Storm History Makes a Strong Roof Non-Negotiable
section_history.body:
Davie has been through the storm gauntlet more than once. Since 1990, the area has experienced nine significant storm events, including three that left lasting marks on the community's homes and infrastructure. Hurricane Andrew in 1992 was a watershed moment for all of South Florida — a Category 5 monster that exposed catastrophic weaknesses in pre-existing roofing systems and directly triggered an overhaul of the Florida Building Code. Davie wasn't spared from Andrew's reach, and the damage it caused revealed just how vulnerable older roofing methods truly were.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 brought its own brand of destruction, tearing through Broward County with sustained winds and a fast-moving forward speed that generated widespread roof damage across the entire county — including neighborhoods throughout Davie. Tarps became a common sight from Rolling Hills to Shenandoah for weeks after Wilma passed. Then came Hurricane Irma in 2017, which served as a sobering reminder that even homes rebuilt or repaired after previous storms aren't automatically safe. Irma caused significant shingle loss, soffit damage, and flashing failures throughout the region.

The pattern is clear: Davie gets hit, and roofs that aren't built to modern hurricane standards suffer the consequences. If your roof was installed before 2005 or hasn't been evaluated by a licensed contractor since Irma, now is the time to act. A properly installed, hurricane-rated roof isn't just about surviving the next storm — it's about protecting everything underneath it for the next 25 to 40 years.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code and Davie Roofing: What Every Homeowner Must Know
section_code.body:
Roofing in Davie is governed by the Florida Building Code (FBC), 8th Edition, with Broward County local amendments that add additional layers of scrutiny to the permitting and inspection process. While Davie is not located within the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — that designation is reserved for Miami-Dade and portions of Broward closer to the coast — homes here are still required to meet stringent hurricane-rated product and installation standards. This is not a technicality; it's a meaningful code requirement that affects which materials can be legally installed and how they must be fastened.

Under FBC requirements for Davie's 170 mph design wind speed zone, roofing systems must be tested and approved to withstand uplift pressures calculated for that wind exposure. This means shingles must carry appropriate Miami-Dade NOA (Notice of Acceptance) or Florida Product Approval designations, and tile systems must be installed using approved mortar, foam adhesive, or mechanical fastening methods that meet or exceed wind resistance standards. Every re-roof project in Davie requires a permit pulled through the Town of Davie Building Division, and each project must pass multiple inspections — including a dry-in inspection and a final inspection — before the permit is closed.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we handle the entire permitting process on your behalf. We prepare documentation, submit applications to the Town of Davie Building Division, and coordinate all required inspections. This isn't optional for us — it's part of how we ensure that your new roof is legally compliant, structurally sound, and eligible for the insurance benefits that come with a properly permitted installation. Homeowners who attempt to use unlicensed contractors or skip the permit process often find themselves paying twice when insurance claims are denied or when it comes time to sell the home.

section_products.h2: CertainTeed Shingles and West Lake Tile: Premium Roofing Built for Davie's Climate
section_products.body:
At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've carefully selected our roofing product lines based on one standard: what performs best in South Florida's demanding environment. For Davie homeowners who prefer the classic, clean look of architectural shingles, we install CertainTeed's Landmark Series — one of the most respected shingle lines in the industry. Landmark Series shingles are engineered for durability, featuring a dual-layered construction that provides enhanced wind resistance and a Class A fire rating. They carry Florida Product Approval and are available in a wide range of colors and profiles to complement any Davie home aesthetic, from traditional ranch-style homes to contemporary two-stories.

For homeowners in premium neighborhoods like Long Lake Ranches or the larger estate properties near Flamingo Gardens who prefer tile — a hallmark of South Florida luxury architecture — we install West Lake concrete and clay tile systems. West Lake tile is manufactured specifically for Florida's climate, offering superior resistance to high winds, UV degradation, and the salt-laden humidity that drifts inland from the coast. Whether you choose the warm earth tones of clay tile or the versatile profiles of concrete tile, West Lake systems provide a roof that's as beautiful as it is bulletproof.

Both product lines are installed by our own in-house crews using manufacturer-approved methods, which is critical for preserving warranty coverage. CertainTeed's SureStart PLUS warranty protection is only available through certified contractors — and Premier Impact Windows & Roofing qualifies. Combined with proper underlayment systems, ice and water barrier application at critical areas, and code-compliant fastening schedules, every roof we install in Davie is built to deliver decades of reliable protection.

section_cost.h2: What Does a New Roof Cost in Davie — and How Do You Finance It?
section_cost.body:
Roofing costs in Davie vary depending on the size of your home, the pitch and complexity of your roof, the materials selected, and the condition of the existing decking underneath. For a typical Davie home — valued around $460,000 and ranging from 2,000 to 3,500 square feet — a full shingle roof replacement using CertainTeed Landmark Series materials generally falls in the range of $15,000 to $25,000. A tile roof replacement with West Lake concrete or clay tile will typically run higher, from $22,000 to $40,000 or more depending on system complexity. These are real-world estimates, not bait-and-switch lowball figures.

The return on that investment is substantial. A new hurricane-rated roof in Davie directly increases your home's appraised value — critical in a market where buyers and appraisers scrutinize roof age and condition closely. Studies consistently show that a new roof returns 60 to 70 cents on the dollar at resale, and in a hot South Florida market, a fresh roof can be the deciding factor that gets your home sold quickly and at full asking price. Add in the insurance premium savings that accompany a wind mitigation inspection (often $1,000 to $3,000+ annually), and your new roof can effectively pay for itself faster than most homeowners expect.

To make sure cost never stands between a Davie homeowner and a safe roof, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing options with approvals starting at $87 per month and $0 down options available for qualified buyers. We work with multiple lending partners to find terms that fit your budget. Ask about our financing options during your free estimate — because the right time to replace your Davie roof is always before the next storm, not after.

section_insurance.h2: How a New Roof in Davie Can Dramatically Lower Your Homeowners Insurance
section_insurance.body:
Florida homeowners insurance has become one of the most pressing financial concerns in the state — and Davie residents are no exception. Many carriers have pulled back from the Florida market entirely, and those that remain are scrutinizing roof age, condition, and material type more aggressively than ever. In Broward County, it's increasingly common for insurers to refuse to write or renew policies on homes with roofs older than 15 to 20 years, regardless of their visible condition. A new roof from Premier Impact Windows & Roofing isn't just a structural upgrade — it's a key to maintaining insurability in an increasingly difficult market.

Beyond policy eligibility, a properly permitted new roof opens the door to significant premium savings through Florida's wind mitigation credit system. After your new roof is installed, a licensed wind mitigation inspector evaluates your home and prepares a report documenting features like roof covering type, deck attachment method, and roof-to-wall connection strength. These credits — which apply directly to the wind portion of your homeowners insurance premium — can reduce annual costs by $1,000 to $3,000 or more for a typical Davie home. When your roof is installed to code and documented correctly, those savings add up year after year.

Common carriers serving Davie homeowners — including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property, and Heritage Insurance — all recognize wind mitigation credits under Florida law. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing coordinates closely with our clients to ensure their roof replacement is documented in the way that maximizes those credits. We recommend scheduling a wind mitigation inspection within 30 days of your final permit closing, and we're happy to guide you through that process from start to finish.

faqs.q: How do I know if my Davie home needs a full roof replacement or just repairs?
faqs.a: A professional inspection is the only reliable way to assess roof condition in Davie. Homes built before 1994 — roughly 40% of the town's housing stock — are especially prone to having outdated sheathing attachment methods and underlayment systems that no longer meet Florida Building Code. Signs that warrant replacement include widespread granule loss, multiple missing shingles, visible sagging, recurring leaks after minor storms, or a roof age exceeding 20 years. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers free inspections and honest assessments — we'll tell you exactly what you need and what can wait.

faqs.q: What does roofing financing look like for Davie homeowners?
faqs.a: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers roofing financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified applicants. We work with multiple lending partners to match you with terms that fit your budget. Financing approval is fast — often same-day — so you don't have to delay a needed roof replacement waiting on funds. Given that a new roof in Davie typically ranges from $15,000 to $40,000 depending on size and material, spreading that cost over manageable monthly payments makes it accessible for most upper-middle income households in the area.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace my roof in Davie, and who handles that process?
faqs.a: Yes — any full roof replacement in Davie requires a permit issued by the Town of Davie Building Division, and the project must pass all required inspections including dry-in and final. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permit process on your behalf. We prepare all required documentation, submit applications, and coordinate inspections to keep your project on schedule. Working without a permit is illegal and can result in fines, mandatory tear-offs, insurance claim denials, and complications when selling your home.

faqs.q: How long does a roof replacement take in Davie?
faqs.a: Most standard shingle roof replacements on Davie homes can be completed in one to two days once materials are staged and permits are approved. Tile roof replacements typically take two to four days depending on the size and complexity of the roof. Permit processing through the Town of Davie Building Division generally takes one to three weeks, though timelines can vary by season and inspection demand. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides realistic project timelines upfront and keeps you informed throughout the process so there are no surprises.

faqs.q: Will a new roof actually lower my homeowners insurance premium in Davie?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes — significantly. Florida's wind mitigation credit system allows homeowners with qualifying roof systems to receive substantial discounts on the wind portion of their insurance premium. For a typical Davie home, these credits can translate to $1,000 to $3,000 or more in annual savings. Additionally, many carriers in Broward County will not insure or renew policies on roofs older than 15 to 20 years, meaning a new roof may also restore your insurability with preferred carriers offering more competitive rates.

cta_headline: Ready to Protect Your Davie Home with a Hurricane-Rated Roof?
cta_body: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is ready to inspect your home, walk you through your options, and deliver the honest, expert service that Davie homeowners deserve — contact us today for your free estimate and discover why we're the Kings of Service in Broward County.
